name: Hal Galper
abstract: Harold Galper (April 18, 1938 – July 18, 2025) was an American jazz pianist, composer, arranger, bandleader, educator, and writer.

interviews.title: Hal Galper interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 27, 2022

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Jazz and The American Song

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: piano players, it was first was Mike Mellilo, then it was Hal Galper, then Jim McNeely, and then Bill Charlap.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jamey Aebersold
about_quotes.evidence: you had people like Hal Galper who you’ve interviewed... I’m not going to give it to you. Kids at the camp. “Hal, how’d you do that? Did you record it?” “No. Well find somebody to record it and take it off. I’m not going to tell you.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jamey Aebersold interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 26, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Roy McCurdy
about_quotes.evidence: He was one of the wonderful piano players that came through, Joe Zawinul, George Duke, Hal Galper... But he was our last piano player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roy McCurdy interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995
